$comment: RATCHET FLOORS, not aspirational 100%. Each floor is the whole-number percentage below the last measured value (see enforcement.measured) so the gate is GREEN today and BLOCKS regression. This is the single source of truth: scripts/web/coverage.sh (tarpaulin), scripts/web/validate-llvm-coverage.mjs (llvm-cov), and .github/workflows/ci.yml (CI llvm-cov line gate) all READ it. Raise floors as coverage improves; never lower a floor without a documented justification (that is the ratchet). The old 100% values were fiction — nothing read this file and no automated context ran the gate (see docs/ci/coverage-gate-findings.md).

enforcement.ci: cargo llvm-cov --workspace --all-features --fail-under-lines <thresholds.llvm.lines> — enforced in .github/workflows/ci.yml (the primary product-coverage gate). Uses llvm-cov (source-based), NOT tarpaulin: tarpaulin's ptrace engine hangs on this workspace under CI.
enforcement.localComposite: scripts/web/coverage.sh — tarpaulin (thresholds.tarpaulin.lines, --timeout 300) + llvm-cov floors (thresholds.llvm.*) + JS-tooling self-coverage (thresholds.jsTooling.* via c8). Requires the pinned toolchain; runs locally.
enforcement.ratchet: A floor MUST NOT be lowered without a written justification committed alongside the change. Prefer raising floors toward the measured values as coverage improves.

measured.justification: Branches floor lowered 82 -> 81 with owner approval: PR #105 (governance slice 2) and #106 (WU-002b) merged AFTER the 13:09 recalibration (55e953a) and added ~212 branches at low branch coverage, putting main itself at 81.64% — below the floor they predated. The regression is theirs, not the measuring branch's (WU-001N adds exactly one uncovered branch, a test-helper let-else panic arm). The governance lane still owes branch tests to earn the floor back to 82+. Earlier recalibration context: the measured Rust workspace expanded from 9,571 tarpaulin lines on 2026-07-15 to 19,887 after the transport, anchor, client networking, and followed-site work landed; no product code is excluded.

enforcement.description: jsTooling covers the scripts/web/**/*.mjs tooling's OWN tests (c8 --all), orthogonal to product coverage; it stays at 100 because that tooling is small and fully tested. Product coverage is Rust: tarpaulin lines + llvm lines/functions/regions/branches.
